rulings.Case Title: JERRY M PARKER VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Dismiss For Failure To Serve Complaint Within Two Years
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 3, 2009, line 4, DEFENDANT ASHBY LUMBER COMPANY MOTION to Dismiss for Failure to Serve Complaint Within Two Years. Motion denied. rulings.Case Title: JON SIEGAL VS. SOLIDUS NETWORKS INC A CORPORATION DOING et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 3, 2009, line 6, DEFENDANT SOLIDUS NETWORKS, INC. DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend. Plaintiff to specifically plead facts relied on that relate to the promises made, when the promises were made, who made the promises, the targets established, and the manner in which the parties were required to perform. Motion to strike denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: RONALD CROSLEY et al VS. CITY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Defts Mtn For Summ Judgment Or In The Alt Mtn For Summ Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 3, 2009, line 21, DEFENDANT CARLOS FAJARDO, and, CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO Motion for Summmary Judgment or in the Alternative Summary Adjudication. Motion for summary adjudication granted as to plaintiff's dangerous condition claim. Motion for summary judgment and summary adjudication on remaining issues denied. Defendants shift burden. Triable issue of fact raised whether driver acted negligently when he hit decedent based on the Crosley and Jones declarations. All objections overruled, although audio evidence not considered. Request for sanctions denied. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ANNE W THAYER VS. CENTRAL CALIFORNIA BANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Reconsideration
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 3, 2009, line 22, PLAINTIFF ANNE THAYER Motion for Reconsideration. Motion for reconsideration denied as untimely and procedurally infirm. Court lacks jurisdiction under APRI Ins. Co. vs. Superior Court (1999) 76 Cal.App.4th 176 and CCP section 581d. No basis for motion for new trial under CCP section 657. Insufficient showing of irregularity in the proceedings, accident or surprise, newly discovered evidence, or insufficiency of the evidence. Commissioner Borick's order and action irrelevant to this law and motion department. Commissioner Borick's Order to Show Cause was issued pursuant to the Trial Court Delay Reduction Act to move the case along and did not modify the law and motion order. Plaintiff appeared at ex parte hearing and argued the merits thereby waiving any defects or irregularities in the notice. (Carlton vs. Quint(2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 690.) =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: BANK OF AMERICA, NATIONAL ASSOCIATION VS. RICHARD FAGAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Strike Of Deft David Fagan To Complain For Unlawful Detainer;
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, September 3, 2009, line 20, DEFENDANT DAVID FAGAN Motion to Strike Complaint for Unlawful Detainer. Motion denied, five days leave to respond to complaint. Verification sufficient and achieves the object of assuring good faith in the averments or statements of the party. (Frio vs. Supeior Court (1988) 203 Cal.App.3d 1480.) Any defect is one of form which does not affect the substance. (Soltani-Rastegar vs. Superior Court (1989) 208 Cal.App.3d 424.) =(302/CWW)
